name: GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop
name_original: Mirador de Jarapalos y Hoya de Málaga – circular por la senda Presidiarios (SL-A 223)
description: This difficult 12.1-mile jogging loop in Sierra de Mijas offers challenging terrain and 2498 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Alhaurín De La Torre offers diverse natural landscapes, from challenging mountain trails to scenic river paths and urban green spaces. The region is situated at the northern foot of the Sierra de Mijas, providing significant elevation changes and panoramic views. The Guadalhorce Valley offers flatter, rural trails through orchards and along rivers. Within the town, well-maintained parks provide accessible options for more relaxed running. Best jogging routes around Alhaurín De La Torre The most popular jogging route is GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gains within the Sierra de Mijas. Another top favourite among local runners is Jarapalos Viewpoint – Puerto de la Graja loop from Venta Vázquez, a difficult 10.9 miles (17.6 km) path. This route explores the mountainous terrain around the Jarapalos area, offering challenging climbs. Local runners also love the Cañada del Lobo Viewpoint – Wind Pass Viewpoint loop from Alhaurín de la Torre, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through the elevated areas of the Sierra de Mijas, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Jogging around Alhaurín De La Torre is defined by the mountainous Sierra de Mijas, the fertile Guadalhorce Valley, and accessible urban green spaces. The network offers options for various ability levels, from demanding trail running to easier park routes. The routes in Alhaurín De La Torre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Alhaurín De La Torre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Alhaurín De La Torre?
A: There are over 40 running routes around Alhaurín De La Torre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from challenging mountain trails to more relaxed paths through green spaces.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Alhaurín De La Torre?
A: The region offers diverse terrain. You can expect challenging mountain trails with significant elevation gains in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996221572/gr-249-2-and-presidiarios-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; provides flatter, scenic rural trails through orchards and along rivers. Within the town, there are also well-maintained urban parks and green spaces for more relaxed runs.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: While many routes in the Sierra de Mijas are challenging, the &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; offers flatter, more accessible paths. Additionally, Alhaurín De La Torre has well-maintained urban parks like the Municipal Park and Parque La Zambrana, which are ideal for relaxed jogging or beginners.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: You'll encounter diverse natural beauty. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt; offers impressive views and the Jarapalos Arboretum. The &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; features fields of olive, almond, and citrus trees, and the Guadalhorce River itself. For specific points of interest, consider routes that pass near highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6109990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Bull&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7268549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock (Mijas)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Alhaurín De La Torre are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21831484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarapalos Viewpoint – Puerto de la Graja loop from Venta Vázquez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21587631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada del Lobo Viewpoint – Wind Pass Viewpoint loop from Alhaurín de la Torre&lt;/a&gt; are popular loop options in the mountainous areas.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alhaurín De La Torre?
A: The running routes in Alhaurín De La Torre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from demanding mountain trails to scenic river paths, and the beautiful natural landscapes that offer an immersive connection with nature.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths?
A: For family-friendly options, consider the less strenuous paths in the &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; with its rural trails and orchards, or the well-maintained urban parks within Alhaurín De La Torre, which provide safe and accessible environments for a family jog or stroll.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Many natural trails in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; are generally suitable for dogs, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific parks or protected areas, and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb wildlife or other users.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Alhaurín De La Torre?
A: Alhaurín De La Torre offers pleasant conditions for running for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal with mild temperatures. Even in winter, the weather is often suitable. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt; is perfect for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14592973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wolf Viewpoint – Trail from Monte Calamorro loop from Alhaurín de la Torre&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, providing a demanding workout with rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Yes, many routes, especially those in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt;, offer spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21831484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarapalos Viewpoint – Puerto de la Graja loop from Venta Vázquez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21587631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada del Lobo Viewpoint – Wind Pass Viewpoint loop from Alhaurín de la Torre&lt;/a&gt; are specifically named for their scenic overlooks, providing impressive vistas of the Sierra and the coast.

Q: Are there any places to explore near the running trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore various attractions. Consider visiting historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Alcazaba of Málaga and the Roman Theatre&lt;/a&gt;, or natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6109990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Bull&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce River&lt;/strong&gt; mouth is also a nature reserve rich in biodiversity, perfect for observation after a run.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging route is approximately 19.5 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 42 minutes to complete when jogging. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, featuring ancient footpaths, wide forest tracks, and cobbled sections. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, including ascents up mountains and descents on footpaths through forests and gravel tracks. Expect a challenging mix of surfaces suitable for experienced joggers.

faqs.question: Is the GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult due to its mountainous nature and substantial elevation changes. It is best suited for experienced joggers or those seeking a significant challenge. Beginners might find the 761 meters of elevation gain and varied terrain too demanding.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: A convenient parking option is near the Real Sociedad de Tiro de Pichón Jarapalo Entrance. This is a common starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the northern side of the Sierra de Mijas, offering dramatic and diverse scenery. You'll pass through areas with large pine trees, kermes oaks, and remnants of holm oak forests. Keep an eye out for notable geological features like dolostones, which are grey-cut marble formations. The northern slopes tend to be shadier and more forested, providing a refreshing experience.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop offers several stunning vistas. You'll pass by the Viewpoint of Hoya de Málaga and Guadalhorce Valley, providing expansive views. Another key spot is the Jarapalos Viewpoint, which offers a unique perspective of the Sierra de Mijas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The northern slopes of the Sierra de Mijas, where much of this trail lies, are generally shadier and more forested. This makes it more pleasant during warmer months. However, for jogging, spring and autumn offer the most comfortable temperatures. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if jogging during that season.

faqs.question: Is the GR 249.2 and Presidiarios Trail Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact loop are not detailed, trails within the Sierra de Mijas are generally dog-friendly. It is always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult, long-distance route with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Sierra de Mijas?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Sierra de Mijas, including this loop. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit, especially for any protected areas or specific sections of the park.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. A significant portion (68%) follows the Variante GR 249.2 Puerto de los Pescadores - Alhaurín de la Torre. It also includes segments of the Presidiarios trail (24%) and parts of the Mijas - Benalmádena trail (19%).

faqs.question: Are there any water sources or places to cool off along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the northern slopes of the Sierra de Mijas feature perennial springs such as Acebuche, La Piedra, or Jarapalos. These can offer opportunities to cool off or refill water bottles, but it's always advisable to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of this challenging route.
